title: Swing z-buffer occlusion: two-sided-fold family detector + depth hook
goal: Swing's fold DIRECTION is now correct (14.44 dB, T-q00deefab anchor Top<->Bottom flip). REMAINING: mid-transition f12-f16 (~10.5-11.4 dB) over-occlude — the outgoing Clone A (Transition A) is painted OVER the incoming Clone B in painter's/reverse order, but at those frames B should win the upper pixels (it has smaller world-Z / is more face-on). The per-pixel Z-buffer helpers projectQuadWithWorldZ + renderPerspectiveQuadDepth (engine/src/compositor/perspective.ts, unit-tested) are ready to hook in renderChildLayers: allocate a shared Float64Array zbuf=Infinity for a group whose visible children are all perspective clones, render each via renderPerspectiveQuadDepth in place of the reverse-list painter loop. BLOCKER for no-hardcode: the naive detector '2 clone children, one perpendicular rotationX' fires ONLY on Swing (corpus-verified: no other built-in has a static-perpendicular 2-clone group). Must generalize the two-sided-fold key to fire on >=2 built-ins BEFORE hooking — candidates: broaden to any group whose visible children are >=2 perspective quads with real world-Z spread (would also catch Reflection/Rotate/3D_Rectangle depth stacks). Then gate: the depth composite must NET-improve Swing (prior WIP measured a NAIVE center-Z paint at 12.58 and per-pixel at 12.63 on the OLD wrong geometry; re-measure on the NEW correct-direction geometry — it should now help since B genuinely occupies the near/top region). Cite: perspective.ts SWING DECODE block + evaluator adjustAnchorFoldDirection.
